spec_match.rationale: Touches key-value, API, crashReport, crash-reporting from the chromestatus summary but skips WICG — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, input); controls include "Next step →"; "Set key"; "Reset all" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and some failure/feature-detect paths are surfaced, but the full set of edge cases (timeouts, abort, malformed input, multiple invocations) is not exercised.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6228675846209536 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.reset-btn:hover).

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on session-journal
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Crash Reporting key-value API introduces WICG but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full. Spec: https://github.com/WICG/crash-reporting/pull/37

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on session-journal
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring session-journal back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: button rule overrides background without color (.reset-btn:hover). See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: session-journal covers the happy path of Crash Reporting key-value API and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions.
